<pre>
Ramon benchmark for Z3
-
Job description: QF_LIA, lp.lia_weighted_scheduling=true (weighted scheduling)
Job tag: lia_w_on_qflia_60_rs_603
Runner: rise-runner-2
Z3 repo: Z3Prover/z3
Z3 commit: 058c900ea2fa6d807194dd31752173459d794b5c
Z3 branch: lia_w
Z3 options: "-T:60 smt.random_seed=603 lp.lia_weighted_scheduling=true"
Z3 inputs: https://zenodo.org/records/16740866/files/QF_LIA.tar.zst?download=1
Z3 commit message: lp: schedule integer-solving methods by weighted random policy (optional)

Replace the fixed-priority waterfall in int_solver::imp::check with a
weighted roulette-wheel selection over the candidate methods (int_cube,
largest cube, HNF cut, Gomory cut, Diophantine equations).

Each method starts at a per-method base weight; on every check one enabled
method is drawn with probability proportional to its weight, a failure
(lia_move::undef) halves the weight (floor of 1) and a success resets it to
the base. cube and lcube are cheap (no move-to-bounds) and start with a
higher base weight (4096) than the post-move methods hnf/gomory/dio (1024),
so the expensive move_non_basic_columns_to_bounds() is favored less.

This unifies the previously ad-hoc per-method period-doubling logic
(find_lcube, hnf_cut, solve_dioph_eq) into a single decay rule while keeping
every method occasionally reachable.

The new policy is gated by the lp.lia_weighted_scheduling parameter
(default true). Setting it to false restores the original fixed-priority
waterfall (run_waterfall_methods) for A/B benchmarking. solve_dioph_eq keeps
its conflict side effects and the period counter that enables Gomory/GCD.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

</pre>
